steps.title: List the two data sets
steps.content: For Foothill, the internship counts are $87$, $80$, $75$, $76$, and $70$. For Valley, the counts are $44$, $54$, $65$, $76$, and $82$.

steps.title: Compare the means
steps.content:
Compute each mean by adding the five values and dividing by $5$.
$$\frac{87+80+75+76+70}{5}=\frac{388}{5}=77.6$$
$$\frac{44+54+65+76+82}{5}=\frac{321}{5}=64.2$$
Since $77.6>64.2$, statement I is true.

steps.title: Compare the medians
steps.content:
Order each set and use the middle value.
$$70,75,76,80,87$$
$$44,54,65,76,82$$
The Foothill median is $76$, and the Valley median is $65$. Since $76>65$, statement II is true.

steps.title: Choose the matching statement
steps.content: Both statements are true, so choose the option that includes I and II. The answer is C.
